channel.title: Frontaliere Ticino — English
channel.description: News and guides for cross-border workers in Ticino

item.title: Europe Takes Action: Spain Cuts Fuel Prices by 30 Cents per Litre
item.description: Spain Becomes First European Country to Cut Fuel Prices Amid Rising Energy Costs.

item.title: Mendrisio trial: 19 charges for a 'criminal entity'
item.description: A 37-year-old man faces deportation after appearing before the Mendrisio Criminal Court on 19 counts ranging from complicity in embezzlement to brawling

item.title: Switzerland-Italy Congress in Varese: tax updates for Ticino cross-border workers
item.description: On 16 April 2026 at Villa Andrea, experts and institutions will present the latest tax, pension and regulatory developments for those who live and work between Italy and Switzerland.

item.title: Low Mendrisiotto merger at risk: debt stalls Terre di Breggia
item.description: After 2.5 years of talks, the merger plan between Balerna, Breggia, Chiasso, Morbio and Vacallo founders on Chiasso’s 140-million debt. Rizza (Vacallo) demands guarantees, Arrigoni (Chiasso) replies: «We already cut 3 million a year». The Canton weighs suspension.

item.title: Newborn tests, Ticino left out of Swiss checks
item.description: The Confederation did not screen Ticino infants for contamination: what your child faces and what to do

item.title: More rescue operations, but fewer mountain fatalities in 2025
item.description: In 2025, the Swiss Alpine Club (CAS) recorded an increase in rescue operations, yet a decrease in mountain fatalities.

item.title: Convenient Gasoline in Ticino
item.description: Ticinese motorists are looking for the most convenient gasoline in Como

item.title: The Swiss Franc Hits Record Lows Against the Euro Since January
item.description: The Swiss Franc has reached levels of 0.92 francs, a threshold not seen since the end of January

item.title: Gallarate: commuter on e-bike arrested for drug dealing
item.description: A 47-year-old man has been arrested in Gallarate for drug dealing. He used an electric bicycle to get around.

item.title: No more tracks A and B: Ticino middle school to change from 2026
item.description: The State Council has decided: from 2026 there will be no more ability grouping for German and maths. All lower-secondary schools in the Canton of Ticino will adopt the new system after a trial run in six institutes.

item.title: Bossi's Commemoration Turns into Brawl in Council
item.description: The commemoration for the founder of the Lega Umberto Bossi ended in a brawl with shouts and accusations between the majority and opposition.

item.title: Lavena Ponte Tresa Commits to Cleaning and Greening the Area
item.description: On the occasion of the planting of new greenery, the municipality gave away watering cans to businesses to encourage the care of the streets.

item.title: Avs Saronno: 'To the young people, you made a difference'
item.description: The result of the constitutional referendum in Saronno: a strong political signal for the future

item.title: Work in Prison: 15 Positions Available
item.description: The Canton of Ticino is looking for prison guards. Deadline for the call: March 31, 2026.

item.title: Val Calanca, Four Municipalities Explore a Structural Merger
item.description: Aggregation project in Calanca: four municipalities are considering the creation of a stronger entity to face challenges such as depopulation and staff shortages. Discover the details.

item.title: How Much Will the VAT Increase Cost Families in Ticino
item.description: A VAT increase to 9.6% would weigh between 300 and 1,400 francs annually on Ticino families, with impacts varying by income and household composition.

item.title: Unions Against SNL: Wages, Transparency, and Rights in the Spotlight
item.description: Angelo Stroppini (SEV) denounces irregularities and wage disparities at the Società Navigazione Lago di Lugano, highlighting unresolved issues in 2026.

item.title: Compulsory Schooling, Total Ban on Cell Phones Takes Effect in Ticino
item.description: From 2024 in Ticino, compulsory schools ban the use of cell phones both inside classrooms and on school grounds: a measure aimed at promoting concentration and learning.

item.title: Wealthy Fleeing the Middle East: Ticino Risks Missing an Opportunity
item.description: The unstable situation in the Middle East is pushing capital and affluent taxpayers to seek new residences. Canton Ticino must act to attract this wealth.

item.title: Monteviasco Cable Car: New Schedules and More Trips for Enhanced Mobility
item.description: The Monteviasco cable car expands its service to six days a week, providing a reliable connection between Ponte di Piero and the mountain village. Discover the schedules and benefits for residents and tourists.

item.title: A Ticino native to head two important military services
item.description: Colonel SMG Stefano Trojani will take over as head of the Military Intelligence Service and the Preventive Protection Service of the army as of June 1, 2026.

item.title: The Council of Europe Assesses Switzerland and Ticino's Progress in Combating Violence Against Women
item.description: On March 11, the Council of Europe monitored Switzerland and Ticino's progress in addressing gender-based violence.

item.title: Ban on mobile phones in compulsory schools in Ticino
item.description: From March 30, 2026, Ticino extends the ban on mobile phone use in compulsory schools, involving all educational institutions.

item.title: Inflated Medical Bills: An Increasingly Common Problem in Ticino
item.description: In Ticino, 60% of patients have received incorrect medical bills, impacting care costs and insurance premiums.

item.title: Clash Over Supplemental Healthcare in Lombardy: 27 Organizations Oppose
item.description: Twenty-seven organizations against agreements between public and private sectors in Lombardy. Risk of privileged access and reduced transparency.

item.title: Ticino Navigation: Enhanced Offers for 2026
item.description: Lugano Lake Navigation is preparing for a new season with enhanced offers, aiming to become a leading tourist carrier.

item.title: Smartphone Ban in Ticino Schools: New Measures
item.description: Starting March 30, Ticino extends the smartphone ban to all compulsory schools to protect the health of young people.

item.title: The Low VAT in Switzerland: A Misleading Image
item.description: Despite the rate of 8.1%, experts warn: the Swiss tax system is complex and should not be oversimplified.

item.title: The Manifesto for Health Welfare is Born in Varese
item.description: A new manifesto for welfare in Varese: fifteen pillars for the voice of patients in public health.

item.title: Increase in diesel prices: 2.10 francs in Switzerland
item.description: Diesel prices continue to rise in Switzerland, reaching 2.10 francs per liter due to the international crisis.

item.title: Chocolate in Switzerland: Decreased Consumption but Rising Prices
item.description: In 2025, chocolate consumption in Switzerland declined, but revenue grew by 11.8%, amid significant cost pressures.

item.title: High Rents: National Council Rejects Rent Control
item.description: The National Council rejects proposals for greater control over rents, raising concerns about the housing crisis.

item.title: BYD expands in Ticino: 50 dealerships by 2026
item.description: The Chinese manufacturer BYD aims for 50 dealerships in Switzerland by 2026, with two already operational in Ticino.

item.title: Inspections at Construction Sites in Mendrisiotto: Positive Outcome
item.description: Seven inspections at construction sites in Mendrisiotto revealed no irregularities, confirming the compliance of workers.

item.title: The Pharmaceutical Industry: For Europe to be Competitive, it Must Have Clear and Predictable Rules
item.description: Italian citizens over 75 years old consume three times more medication than the average of the rest of the population

item.title: Fuel tax cut puts pressure on Ticinese distributors
item.description: The measure, decided by Rome and valid for 20 days, provides a 25-cent discount per liter. The Ticinese category association is concerned and is asking for a political intervention.

item.title: The Swiss Franc at Record Values Makes Frontaliers Richer, but an Announcement Can Change Everything
item.description: The Swiss franc has reached record values, making frontaliers richer, but the announcement of the Swiss National Bank may change everything.

item.title: The Transit Tax Could Work Like the Pedemontana: Who, What, Where, When, Why
item.description: The transit tax could be introduced in Switzerland to discourage traffic that crosses the country without bringing direct economic benefits. National Councillor UDC Paolo Pamini has proposed an automatic electronic taxation system, similar to that used by the Pedemontana between Como and Malpensa.

item.title: Delays on the Lines to Milan: Fault Between Parabiago and Rho
item.description: Disruptions on the railway line to Milan: possible cancellations and changes for various Lombardy connections

item.title: Globalists' Exodus from the Middle East: An Opportunity for Ticino?
item.description: Ticino could benefit from the exit of significant wealth from the Middle East, but a clear strategy is needed.

item.title: Renewal of concessions and expansion of services: SNL's plans for 2026
item.description: The Società Navigazione del Lago di Lugano (SNL) will present its plans for the renewal of concessions and expansion of services for 2026. New connections, themed experiences, and large events on Lake Maggiore and Lake Ceresio will be at the center of attention.

item.title: Shock Calls in Ticino: Two Arrests for Elderly Scams
item.description: Two Polish nationals were detained on March 9 in the Canton of Ticino, accused of carrying out scams targeting seniors through shock calls in Chiasso and Biasca.

item.title: The man who changed Italian politics: Bossi's deep affection for Ticino
item.description: The founder of the Northern League remembered by Ticino journalists Norman Gobbi and Lorenzo Quadri. A man who left a profound mark on Italian political history.

item.title: Switzerland Introduces Transit Tax, EU Commission Objects
item.description: The Swiss government has introduced a transit tax to reduce traffic and improve quality of life for residents, but the EU Commission is not pleased with the decision.

item.title: Ticino: Federal Contribution for Restoration of Agrarian Structures Damaged by the 2024 Flood in the Grisons Region
item.description: The Grisons government grants 511,000 Swiss francs for the restoration of agrarian structures damaged by the 2024 flood in the Grisons region.

item.title: Home-care levy: the new tax splitting politicians and citizens
item.description: Introduced on 1 April 2026, Ticino’s new home-care tax is sparking mixed reactions among politicians and residents.

item.title: Fuel prices sky-high: Italy steps in while Switzerland does nothing
item.description: Italy has cut fuel excise duties, whereas Switzerland has taken no action.

item.title: San Gottardo, towards the wind-farm expansion: here are the latest developments
item.description: The San Gottardo wind farm could grow: here's what it means for the region.

item.title: Healthcare, Fontana and Fedriga: 'Hospital-centrism is outdated'
item.description: The presidents of Lombardy and Friuli Venezia Giulia discuss the sustainability of the healthcare system in Varese.

item.title: Porrentruy to restrict access to municipal pool for non-residents
item.description: The Porrentruy Municipal Council has approved a legal amendment allowing the Municipal Council to impose restrictions on access to the outdoor municipal pool.
